description: Per-boundary evidence: position, hardness, sharpness, boundary type, supporting cheats, and verdict. Written twice per candidate (boundary_5prime, boundary_3prime). Sharpness is real Hudson Fst/Hobs step from Cheat 4 when Engine B is available, otherwise sim_mat proxy. NAMING: 5prime/3prime are relative to the project reference assembly orientation (bp1 = lower coordinate = 5prime; bp2 = higher coordinate = 3prime). This is NOT relative to NCBI or any external reference. Legacy consumers reading q3_left_*/q3_right_* keys are supported via a dual-write shim in utils/registry_key_helpers.R (flag RKH_EMIT_LEGACY_SIDE_KEYS).
question: Q3

hardness.description: 0-1: how consistent the boundary location is across matrix variants

blue_cross_inner.description: derived from PHASE_01C block_detect inner boundary

note_on_side_templating: The library substitutes {side} with '5prime' or '3prime' at key extraction time based on block_type. So boundary_5prime.json writes q3_5prime_bp, q3_5prime_hardness, etc., and boundary_3prime.json writes q3_3prime_*. During migration, utils/registry_key_helpers.R also dual-writes q3_left_*/q3_right_* copies (toggle RKH_EMIT_LEGACY_SIDE_KEYS). 5prime ≡ bp1 ≡ lower coordinate on the project reference assembly.
